resources.string: Connect the local API and static token to render the home placement at the top of the page.
Story bar placement stays pinned to the top of Home.
Use this screen to validate the fixed story bar entry point, callback wiring, and refresh behavior against your local backend.
Dark chrome keeps the SDK surface isolated. Home renders the live placement first, while Search and List give the sample app enough surrounding navigation to feel like a real host app.
This screen is intentionally static. It gives the bottom navigation realistic context without adding host-side behavior outside the SDK scope.
Feed cache stays scoped by placement, platform, app version, and normalized segments.
On 401 or 403, cached content must not render again.
CTA handling still belongs to the host app callback, never to SDK navigation.
